checks.a_analysis: The output contains text that is present in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences (like invented section titles or hallucinated text) were identified.

I verified all URLs provided in the output (https://NC.gov, https://ncleg.gov/EnactedLegislation/SessionLaws/HTML/2021-2022/SL2021-180.html, and the N.C.G.S statute link). All are present in the source PDF and are accurate. No phone numbers or emails were present in the source or the output.

The output is missing a massive amount of content from the source PDF. Entire sections and pages are absent, including the Contents (Page 2), Vision, Mission and Priorities (Page 5), Enterprise Functions and Divisions (Pages 6-8), and the Awards and Recognitions chapter (Pages 33-40). The output explicitly includes a metadata entry '_omitted': '32 sections omitted for token budget', which confirms the absence of significant portions of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table of Contents (Page 2), Vision, Mission and Priorities (Page 5), Enterprise Functions and Divisions (Pages 6-8), Awards and Recognitions (Pages 33-40).
checks.b_evidence_output: The output jumps from 'About NCDIT' (Page 4) to 'Budget Report' (Page 14), and from 'Goals' (terminating at Page 22) to 'NC.gov Delivers a Frictionless Customer and Citizen Digital Experience' (Page 41).
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections between element index 4 and 17, and between 18 and 39.

checks.a_analysis: The budget tables present in the output were compared to the source. The 'Budget Expenditures' table correctly lists all 8 categories and percentages. The 'Budget Funding Sources' table correctly lists all 8 categories and percentages. No rows are missing.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The 'Goals' section in the source PDF spans pages 16 to 32. The output conversion for this section stops after the content found on page 22. It omits significant subsections such as 'Customer Experience for other Agencies', 'Optimize and Secure...', 'Promote an Inclusive...', 'Data Linking', 'Data Analysis Solutions', 'Privacy', and 'Health Care', which comprise more than half of the chapter's content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Content from pages 23 ('Customer Experience for other Agencies') through 32 ('Health Care').
checks.b_evidence_output: The section 'Goals' (reading_order 18) ends with the 'Emerging Technologies' content from page 22.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Extracted text and data (such as the 99 percent population coverage statistic and various budget percentages) match the source document exactly. No meaningful wording changes were observed in the converted text.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the document for duplicated content. While some headings and phrases repeat (e.g., 'Foster a connected North Carolina...'), this repetition is present in the source PDF (once as a goal summary box on page 16 and again as a section header on page 18). The output correctly reflects this without creating unintended duplicates of paragraphs or lists.

The heading hierarchy is semantic and logical. Major report sections use H2, while sub-sections use H3. The conversion correctly identifies the primary headers on each page. Normalization of heading levels for accessibility (e.g., promoting section headers to H2) is consistent with the 'Acceptable Changes' criteria.

The document contains several complex structural elements. The pie charts on pages 14 and 15 have been converted into tables to ensure screen reader accessibility. I verified the data points (categories and percentages) against the source callouts, and they match perfectly. The 'Broadband By The Numbers' sidebar was also correctly converted into a table, preserving the relationship between the metrics and descriptions.

The reading order follows the page-by-page logical flow of the source PDF. Sidebar content (such as 'NCDIT's 1,300 employees' on page 4 or the 'Broadband By The Numbers' stats on page 19) is placed logically within their respective sections. The omission of the static Table of Contents page is an standard accessibility optimization and does not constitute a reading order defect.

Lists throughout the document (e.g., responsibilities on page 4, goal summaries on pages 16-17, and detailed project lists on pages 19-20) are correctly captured as unordered lists. The nesting and itemization match the source material.

I verified several links in the document, including 'N.C.G.S. 143B, Article 15' (page 4), 'Session Law 2021-180' (page 19), and 'NC.gov' (pages 20, 41). All links are present where indicated in the source and point to the correct, relevant government URLs. No fabricated or broken links were found.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization and flow of the original document, converting the multi-column PDF layout into a single-column responsive web design. The sequence of sections (Secretary's Message, About NCDIT, Goals, etc.) matches the source PDF exactly. Navigation elements like the Table of Contents have been correctly integrated. While styling differs (fonts, background colors), the information is logically placed and easily findable.

Tables representing the 'Budget Report' data (Expenditures and Funding Sources) are rendered correctly. They use a standard row/column structure with clear headers and aligned data cells, making the percentage values easy to read and understand. This is a successful conversion from the pie charts in the source PDF to an accessible table format.

Meaningful images, including photos of leadership and technical diagrams, are represented by placeholder green boxes in the primary layout. While the photos themselves are absent, the technical diagrams (such as the NC eLink Utility Modules on page 29 and the governance structure on page 32) have had their content converted into structured, readable text within the HTML body, which preserves the informational value. Essential logos and some header graphics are present. Given the thorough text-based replacement of diagrams for accessibility, this is considered an acceptable conversion strategy.

There is a major content duplication issue. The entire report content is rendered once in the primary styled layout and then repeated in its entirety as a plain text block at the bottom of the page. This second instance includes everything from the cover page titles and Table of Contents through to the final 'Looking Ahead' section paragraphs.
checks.b_evidence_source: The N.C. Department of Information Technology adopted a new strategic plan for fiscal years 2021-23. This plan outlines our five key goals we are working toward to carry out our mission to enable trusted business-driven solutions that meet the needs of North Carolinians.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot and OCR text show the entire document content starting over from '2021/2022 Annual Report' and the 'Secretary's Message' text immediately following the end of the primary 'Looking Ahead' section (page 41).
checks.b_evidence_location: End of the HTML document, following the final page 41 footer.
